section_title: If the bailee, without the consent of the bailor, mixes the goods of the
content:
157. If the bailee, without the consent of the bailor, mixes the goods of the
mixture,
bailor with his own goods, in such a manner that it is impossible to separate
without
bailor's the goods bailed from the other goods and deliver them back, the bailor is
consent,
entitled to be compensated by the bailee for the loss of the goods.
when the
goods
Illustration
cannot be
separated A bails a barrel of Cape flour worth Taka 45 to B. B, without A's consent,
mixes the flour with country flour of his own, worth only Taka 25 a barrel. B
must compensate A for the loss of his flour.
Repayment
